scientific_integrity.detail: This skill produces structured calculation or lookup outputs rather than empirical research findings, so scientific integrity review is not applicable.

practice_boundaries.detail: The skill operates within clinical/medical data processing scope and does not generate clinical or diagnostic decision outputs.

methodological_ground.detail: No experimental methodology claims are made; the skill generates deterministic structured calculation or lookup outputs without asserting scientific conclusions.

code_usability.detail: Any code produced by this skill is utility-oriented, not bioinformatics analysis code; research veto code review is not applicable.

functional_suitability.note: Covers BMI, BSA (4 formulas), drug dosing, and pediatric mode; Yu formula listed but not in CLI params

reliability.note: Input range validation documented; fallback for missing params; script failure fallback present

performance_context.note: SKILL.md 186 lines — lean; references/ files listed but not bundled

agent_usability.note: Clear workflow, formula selection rationale, and output requirements; WHO classification table is excellent; mixed-request handling now documented

human_usability.note: Description is highly discoverable; clinical note is prominent and appropriate

security.note: No hardcoded secrets; input range validation present; no injection vectors

maintainability.note: Clean separation between calculator module and CLI; references/ files planned but absent

agent_specific.note: Trigger precision excellent; escape hatches for diagnosis/dietary advice present; references/ absent; Yu formula doc/impl mismatch

assertions.note: Input Validation now instructs: complete the calculation and decline only the out-of-scope portion for mixed requests

key_strengths: Comprehensive formula coverage (DuBois, Mosteller, Haycock, Gehan-George, Yu) with clear use-case guidance per formula
Excellent clinical safety design: prominent disclaimer, chemotherapy double-check warning, and scope refusal for diagnosis
Pediatric mode with age-appropriate norms and correct formula selection (Haycock for children < 12)
Input range validation (weight 2–300 kg, height 50–250 cm) prevents physiologically implausible calculations
Mixed-request handling now documented: skill completes in-scope calculation and declines only the out-of-scope portion

recommendations.title: References directory listed but absent from bundle
recommendations.problem: SKILL.md references four files (bsa_formulas_comparison.md, pediatric_norms.md, chemotherapy_dosing.md, ethnic_adjustments.md) that do not exist in the skill directory.
recommendations.root_cause: References were planned but not created, leaving dead links that reduce trust and agent usability.
recommendations.fix: Create the four reference files. chemotherapy_dosing.md and ethnic_adjustments.md are highest priority given clinical safety implications.

recommendations.title: Yu formula documented but not exposed in CLI
recommendations.problem: The Yu formula for Asian populations is listed in the BSA Formulas table but is not available as a CLI option or in the calculate_bsa() call signature.
recommendations.root_cause: Documentation was written ahead of implementation.
recommendations.fix: Either add Yu formula to the CLI --formula parameter and calculator implementation, or remove it from the documentation until implemented.
